Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log in a table named event_log with columns: case_id, activity, timestamp. We want to return only the events from cases that have at most 6 events (i.e., 6 or fewer events in the case).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps: 1. We can first count the number of events per case_id. 2. Then, we can filter to keep only those case_ids where the count is <= 6. 3. Finally, we can join back to the original table to get all the events for these case_ids.

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However, note that we are asked to return the full set of events for the remaining cases.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can do this in two ways: Option 1: Using a subquery to get the case_ids with count <= 6 and then filter the main table. Option 2: Using a window function to count per case and then filter. Lets go with Option 1 for clarity.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step-by-step: Step 1: Get the case_ids that have 6 or fewer events. Step 2: Select all columns from event_log where case_id is in the set from Step 1. Alternatively, we can use a JOIN.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH case_counts AS ( SELECT case_id, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT(*) <= 6 ) SELECT e.* FROM event_log e INNER JOIN case_counts c ON e.case_id = c.case_id;

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively, we can use a subquery in the WHERE clause: SELECT * FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN ( SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT(*) <= 6 );

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Both are acceptable. However, note that if the same case_id appears in multiple groups (which it shouldnt because we are grouping by case_id) then the IN clause is safe.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me choose the CTE method for better readability.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note: The problem says filters out any cases containing more than six events, so we want to keep cases with <=6 events. We are returning the full set of events for these cases. Let me write the query accordingly.
